adv. S. कुत्र or कुह Pālī, kaham, katthā . Where; whither. 1. kahāṅ yeh, aur kahāṅ woh! Look on this picture, and on that! 2. kahāṅ se ṭapak paṛe? Where in the world have you dropt form? 3. Mhāre sāṛe sahrī mhārī aṛai kaṛai kī bol-gat sun-kar hamāṅ gaṅvār kahan lāg jā saëṅ . Rus. That pimp to his own sister! Because I say aṛe (for yahāṅ ) and kaṛe (for kahāṅ ) he calls me an ignorant boor. 4. Latte chāl kaṛai ham rākheṅ? kit rākheṅ bugchā balmāṅ? Mhārī ṭūṭī chhān baṅdhā balmāṅ! Rus. Wom. Where can I stow my clothes? Where keep my bundle, husband, say? my broken thatch, husband, repair! 5. kahāṅ paṛā phirtā hai? Where are you wandering about? kahāṅ tak, talak, tāīṅ, toṛī, lag, yā loṅ ; Sant. okādhārī , adv. How far; how long; to what degree. kahāṅ se ; Sant. okākhon , adv. Whence; where from. kahāṅ kā kahāṅ, kahāṅ kī kahāṅ . Now here, now there; here today, tomorrow gone.
postpn. (dialec.) = ko, the case-sign of the dat. & acc.

n. m. S. स्कंधधार. 1. A caste of Hindus who draw water, carry doolies, etc.; a dooli bearer. 2. A scullion. Dāsī-karam kahār se nīche . Prov. Service is meaner than the water-carrier's caste. kahār'ī, kahāran ; E. kahārin , n. f. 1. A kahār's wife. 2. The wages of a kahār . Johīṅ utrī ḍolī se maiṅ tum ne vohiṅ Piṭārī merī sab ṭaṭolī, kahāro! Alī kī qasam, maiṅ na dūṅgī kahārī, Samajhyo mujhe tum na bholī, kahāro! Wom. Rangīn. No sooner from my litter stepped I out Than you began to pull my trunks about, By Ali's oath I will not pay a penny, Don't think, kahars! that I am such a ninny.
s.f. A woman of the kahār caste; the wife of a kahār.
s.f. The wife of a kahār ( = kahāran); — the business of a kahār; the wages of a kahār.

s. f. Conversation, discussion, altercation.

def: v. a. S. कथन 1. See بولنا 5, 6, 10, 11. 2. To tell; say. 1. Kyoṅ kahī, aur kyoṅ kahāī . Prov. What made you speak—occasion given for gossip's tongue! 2. Kahne se garche hotī hai, yāroṅ, parāī bāt, Ho ham se to nahīṅ na kaheṅ mūṅh par āī bāt . Mir Taqī. To speak's to make another's what your own was now, Yet words that come upon the lips will pass somehow. 3. Kahe ke sab kuchh, kare ke kuchh nā . Bhoj. Prov. He talks a deal but nothing does. 4. Shurfa, bāt agam kī kahan sunan kī nāheṅ, Jāneṅ so kahveṅ nahīṅ, kaheṅ so jāneṅ nāheṅ . Shurfā Shurfā! ne'er talk or hear about the infinite! Who know are silent, while who know not talk of it. 3. To call; name. Is ne mujhe be-vaqūf kahā . 4. To advise; admonish ( samjhānā , 2). Tore lalā ne mo se chānak pīt karī rī! Lākh kahī, vā ne ek na mānī, woh to kuchh prīt kī rīt na jāne, Barjo, Jasodhā! apne kuṅar ko, maiṅ birhan, merī sās burī rī! Your boy with sudden passion felt with me doth plead, A hundred thousand times I chid, he would not heed, Love's ways he knows not yet: restrain him, Jasodha! Unhappy wretch! hard bears on me my mother-in-law! kahā sunī, kahan sunan ; Rus. kahbat sun- bat , n. f. Words ( an-ban ). Un meṅ āpas meṅ kuchh kahā sunī ho gaī. kahte hī ; Sant. lai torā , adv. 1. So saying; no sooner said; instantly; directly; no sooner said than done. 2. Easily; readily. kah'nā , n. m. 1. A word or phrase; saying. 2. Advice ( sīkh 1). 3. Order; bidding. Āp kā kahnā sir āṅkhoṅ par. kah'an , n. f. See kahnā , n. m. Composition. kahnā sunnā ; Mag. kahal sunal , 1. Dispute; altercation. 2. Influence; consideration. Unkā sarkār meṅ baṛā kahnā sunnā hai. kahnā k., kahā k. , v. a. To do one's bidding; carry out instructions. Māre sardī ke hāth kahnā nahīṅ kartā- kahnā yā kahā mānnā, kahne par chalnā, kahne meṅ rahnā , v. n. To obey. kahnā an-kahnā , Improper language. kahne par jānā , v. a. To heed; regard. kahne ko . adv. For the sake of saying. kahne kī bāteṅ , Mere words. kahne meṅ , adv. Under or subject to one's orders; obedient. kahne-vālā ; A. mutakallim ; See uttam pursh , 2. kahā sunā , n. m. 1. Inappropriate speech; harsh language. Hamārā kahā sunā muāf karnā . 2. Pleading; solicitation. Tumhāre kahe sune se shāyad rāzī ho jāë .

def: n. m. See غضب 1. Rage; fury. qahr-i-darvesh bar jān-i-darvesh . P. Prov. The poor man's rage only hurts himself. 2. Calamity. Gar nāëkā in meṅ koī buḍḍhī hai kahātī, Albattah buṛhāpe pe woh ṭuk rahm hai khātī, Phīki sī purānī sī lagāvaṭ hai jatātī, Par qahr hai voh ham ko zarā khush nahīṅ ātī! Old age. Nazīr. If' mongst the young there be an ancient bawd, Her heart with sympathy has towards me thawed, Stale grown and old attachment she can show, But plague on it! I like her not I trow. 3. Firebrand; incendiary ( āg-lagāū ). Arī ek hai, tū baṛī qahr hai, Kahīṅ qand, misrī, kahīṅ zahr hai . Mīr Hasan. There's none like you, a firebrand great, Nectar and poison alternate. qahr , adj. Excessive; great ( gazab , 2). Terī khātir karūṅ kab tak main dugānā pyārī? Qahr us bāt kā lapkā, tujhe dar gor paṛā! Rangīṅ. qahr kā , adj. Calamitous; ruinous.
more: s. m. Force, severity, chastisement, rage, fury, indignation, vengeance, judgment, calamity. قـﮩر قيامت ḳahr-ḳiyāmat, Great disturbance or commotion (is applied both in panegyrick and satire).

Is jānvar kā yehī qāëdah hai . 3. Rule; formula; law. 4. Regularity; uniformity ( bandobast , 2). 5. Regulation ( qānūn ). 6. Precept; maxim; principle. Umr bhar maiṅ isī qāëdah par chalā . 7. A conventional rule; manners; etiquette. Ashrāfoṅ kā yeh qāëdah nahīṅ ki mūṅh se kah-kar phireṅ . 8. (H. achchhar-dīpkā ; P. bugdādī qāëdah ) A primer; first reading book. Maktab meṅ uske bāp ne lā-kar dīyā biṭhā, Ek qāëdah bhī sāmne us tift ke rakha . Nazīr. 9. ( Gram .) A rule ( sūtr ). 10. ( Geom .) The base of a triangle or parallelogram (S. आधार ). qāëdah bāṅdhṅā, yā muqarrar k. , v. a. 1. To make a rule; subject to rule. 2. To regulate; fix; determine; establish. 3. To prescribe; ordain. 4. To provide; stipulate. qāë…
